records.ety: [LG. snaue, or D. snaauw, from LG. snau a snout, a beak.]

defs: A square-rigged vessel, differing from a brig only in that she has a trysail mast close abaft the mainmast, on which a large trysail is hoisted.

records.ety: [OE. snow, snaw, AS. snāw; akin to D. sneeuw, OS. & OHG. snēo, G. schnee, Icel. snær, snjōr, snajār, Sw. snö, Dan. snee, Goth. snaiws, Lith. snëgas, Russ. snieg', Ir. & Gael. sneachd, W. nyf, L. nix, nivis, Gr. acc. ni`fa, also AS. snīwan to snow, G. schneien, OHG. snīwan, Lith. snigti, L. ningit it snows, Gr. ni`fei, Zend snizh to snow; cf. Skr. snih to be wet or sticky. √172.]

defs: Watery particles congealed into white or transparent crystals or flakes in the air, and falling to the earth, exhibiting a great variety of very beautiful and perfect forms.
notes: ☞ Snow is often used to form compounds, most of which are of obvious meaning; as, snow-capped, snow-clad, snow-cold, snow-crowned, snow-crust, snow-fed, snow-haired, snowlike, snow-mantled, snow-nodding, snow-wrought, and the like.

defs: Fig.: Something white like snow, as the white color (argent) in heraldry; something which falls in, or as in, flakes.
quotes: The field of snow with eagle of black therein.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Snowed (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Snowing.]

defs: To fall in or as snow; -- chiefly used impersonally; as, it snows; it snowed yesterday.
To scatter like snow; to cover with, or as with, snow.
